source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A $5 \times 5$ square is made from $1 \times 1$ tiles, all with the same pattern, as shown. Any two adjacent tiles have the same colour along the shared edge. The perimeter of the $5 \times 5$ square consists of black and white segments of length 1 . What is the smallest possible number of black segments on the perimeter of the
<image1>
$5 \times 5$ square?

image_dependency: question states 'all with the same pattern, as shown', so the tile pattern is supplied by the image rather than the text
solving depends on black/white edge configuration and perimeter segments, which are visual/spatial attributes
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and image_manifest shows an available image
multi_step: the task asks for the 'smallest possible number', indicating an optimization objective rather than direct lookup
the condition 'Any two adjacent tiles have the same colour along the shared edge' must be combined across a 5x5 tiling, requiring propagation of local constraints
solution likely needs both interpretation of the tile pattern from the image and reasoning about how repeated tiles affect the full perimeter
answer_type is numeric with no choices, so the result must be derived rather than selected
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='5', giving a clear final target
the constraint system is well specified in the prompt once the image is available, making the final count checkable
because this is a finite 5x5 construction problem, candidate solutions are in principle checkable by explicit perimeter counting or exhaustive verification

risk_flags: visual pattern not described in text

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantic assets are intact
the problem is highly image-dependent, but the required image is available and registry_passed is true
initial scores are strong, especially for image dependency and verifiability, supporting inclusion in the candidate pool
the noted risks do not remove recoverability: lack of solution steps is acceptable at collection time, and the visual pattern is still accessible through the image
